weekly.headline: The S&P 500 rose +1.4% this week while gold finished lower (-3.0%).
weekly.happened: The Fed held rates in its most divided vote since 1992; Big Tech earnings and 2.0% GDP growth pushed the S&P 500 to a record.
weekly.means_for_you: High oil prices are stoking inflation, keeping interest rates elevated — but strong earnings kept stocks at record highs, rewarding patient investors.
weekly.watch_next_week: Watch the US April jobs report (May 8) and any updates on the Iran ceasefire, as both could quickly move stock and oil prices.
